body:
## Fix Summary

Fixed GameStateMachine state transition failure that prevented the game from transitioning from Idle to Spawning state.

## Root Cause

Two bugs in GameStateMachine:

1. Wrong default state: Constructor initialized to WaitingForPiece (legacy state) instead of Idle. This caused inconsistency with GameplayController which expected Idle.

2. CurrentState setter did not fire event: The property setter directly assigned _state without calling ChangeState(), so OnStateChanged was never fired when using the setter. Changed setter to delegate to ChangeState() for consistent behavior.

3. No guard for same-state transitions: ChangeState() did not check if the new state was the same as the current state, causing redundant event fires.

## Files Modified

- Assets/Game/Scripts/Gameplay/GameStateMachine.cs - Fixed default state to Idle, made setter use ChangeState(), added same-state guard
- Assets/Game/Scripts/Gameplay/GameplayController.cs - Removed redundant CurrentState assignment in Awake() (now handled by constructor)
- Assets/Game/Tests/EditMode/GameplayControllerTests.cs - Updated tests to match corrected behavior (Idle default, Spawning event test)

## Test Results

- Edit Mode: 121 passed, 12 failed (12 failures are pre-existing, unrelated to this fix)
- Play Mode: 116 passed, 1 failed (1 failure is pre-existing Integration_GameOver_EventFiresWithScore, unrelated to this fix)

The 2 GameplayControllerTests that were failing due to the old default state now pass.
